name: gravitee-apim
description: Model Context Protocol server for the Gravitee APIM Management API.
long_description: Gravitee APIM MCP server for the APIM 4.12 Management API v2 APIs surface. It exposes API management tools for MCP-compatible clients.

protocol.title: Protocol
protocol.description: Protocol used to connect to the Management API. Options: https, http.

management_api_host.title: Management API host
management_api_host.description: Host name of the APIM Management API, without protocol or path.

bearer_auth.title: Bearer token
bearer_auth.description: APIM bearer token used to authenticate Management API requests.

tools.name: list_apis
tools.description:
List APIs Get the list of APIs for a specific environment.
The results are paginated.

tools.name: create_api
tools.description:
Create an API ⚠️ Support only v4 API for the moment. ⚠️
Create a new API. For V4 PROXY APIs, provide listeners with HTTP paths and an http-proxy entrypoint, plus endpointGroups with an http-proxy endpoint. Example request body: {"name":"My API","apiVersion":"1.0","definitionVersion":"V4","type":"PROXY","listeners":[{"type":"HTTP","paths":[{"path":"/my-api"}],"entrypoints":[{"type":"http-proxy"}]}],"endpointGroups":[{"name":"default","type":"http-proxy","endpoints":[{"name":"backend","type":"http-proxy","configuration":{"target":"https://backend.example.com"}}]}]}

tools.name: create_api_from_swagger
tools.description:
Import API definition ⚠️ Support only v4 API for the moment. ⚠️
Create an API by importing from an Open API descriptor.
This definition can be retrieved from `GET /environments/{envId}/apis/{apiId}/_export/definition`

tools.name: create_api_from_wsdl
tools.description:
Create API from WSDL

Create a v4 HTTP Proxy API from a WSDL descriptor. The WSDL is converted to OpenAPI and processed through the v4 OAI import pipeline. Payload can be inline WSDL (type: INLINE) or a remote URL (type: URL).

tools.name: create_api_with_import_definition
tools.description:
Import API definition ⚠️ Support only v4 API for the moment. ⚠️
Create an API by importing an API definition.
This definition can be retrieved from `GET /environments/{envId}/apis/{apiId}/_export/definition`

tools.name: create_api_with_import_definition_from_url
tools.description:
Import API definition from a remote URL ⚠️ Support only v4 API for the moment. ⚠️
Create an API by fetching an API definition from a remote URL.
The URL must be permitted by the configured import whitelist.

tools.name: search_apis
tools.description:
Search APIs Search APIs for a specific environment.
The results are paginated and can be sorted.

tools.name: verify_paths
tools.description:
Verify HTTP API paths Verify paths before creating or updating a HTTP API.
This will check paths and hosts (depending on environment domain restrictions), and will check that path is not already used by other APIs in the environment.
The result will indicate if the paths are OK, and give the reason of the failure if they are not (path contains invalid chars, path is already covered by another API,...)

tools.name: verify_hosts
tools.description:
Verify TCP and Kafka API hosts Verify hosts before creating or updating a TCP or Kafka API.
This will check it is not already used by other APIs in the environment.
The result will indicate if the hosts are OK, and give the reason of the failure if they are not.

tools.name: get_api
tools.description:
Get a specific API

Get a specific API.

tools.name: update_api
tools.description:
Update an API

Update a V2 or a V4 API.

Fetch the current API first, modify the complete API body, then submit the full update payload.

tools.name: patch_api
tools.description:
Patch a V4 HTTP Proxy API

Partially update a V4 HTTP Proxy API using JSON Patch (RFC 6902) or JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396). Only the following fields are patchable: name, description, apiVersion, visibility, labels, tags, lifecycleState, categories, groups, analytics, failover, flowExecution, flows, services, resources, endpointGroups,

tools.name: delete_api
tools.description:
Delete an API

Delete a V2 or a V4 API.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: create_api_deployment
tools.description:
Request a deployment to gateway instances Request a deployment for a given API.
An optional deployment label can be given to the requested deployment. Deploy after configuration or plan changes and before starting the API. Example request body: {"deploymentLabel":"release"}

tools.name: get_current_api_deployment
tools.description:
Get the current deployment of an API

Get the current deployment of an API.

tools.name: get_api_metadata
tools.description:
Get metadata pertaining to API

Get the metadata that pertains to an API. Can be global and API metadata.

tools.name: export_api_definition
tools.description:
Export an API

⚠️ Support only v4 API for the moment. ⚠️ Export an API to a JSON file. User should also have these permissions for specific data: - API_DEFINITION[READ] for the API - API_MEMBER[READ] for the API's members - API_PLAN[READ] for the API's plans

tools.name: update_api_with_definition
tools.description:
Update API from definition Update an existing API by importing a Gravitee API definition.
This definition can be retrieved from `GET /environments/{envId}/apis/{apiId}/_export/definition`

tools.name: update_api_from_wsdl
tools.description:
Update API from WSDL descriptor Update an existing API by importing a WSDL descriptor.
The descriptor payload must be a valid WSDL 1.1 document, either as inline content or a remote URL.

tools.name: update_api_with_definition_from_url
tools.description:
Update API from a definition fetched from a remote URL ⚠️ Support only v4 API for the moment. ⚠️
Update an existing API by fetching a Gravitee API definition from a remote URL.
The URL must be permitted by the configured import whitelist.

tools.name: update_api_from_swagger
tools.description:
Update API from OpenAPI descriptor Update an existing API by importing an OpenAPI (Swagger) descriptor.
The descriptor payload must be a valid OpenAPI 2.x or 3.x document in JSON or YAML format.

tools.name: start_api
tools.description:
Start an API

Change the API's status to STARTED.

Start only after the API has been deployed.

tools.name: migrate_api
tools.description:
Migrate an API

⚠️ Experimental endpoints - subject to change Migrate an APIv2 to APIv4.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: promote_api
tools.description:
Promote an API

Promote the API to another environment.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: stop_api
tools.description:
Stop an API

Change the API's status to STOPPED.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.
Stops gateway instances for the API; use only when traffic should stop.

tools.name: transfer_ownership
tools.description:
Transfer the ownership of the API

Transfer the ownership of the API to a user, a group or an api member. Return a 404 HTTP Error if API cannot be found.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: duplicate_api
tools.description:
Duplicate an API

Create a new API from an existing one. Context-path is required for HTTP APIs and host is required for TCP APIs. If one of these fields is missing, then it returns a 400 Bad Request.

tools.name: rollback_api
tools.description:
Rollback an API

Rollback an API to a previous version.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: exposed_entrypoints
tools.description:
Get Exposed API Entrypoints

Get the list of exposed API entry points. This list represents the API's usable entrypoints that can be used by a client. It takes into account tags associated with the API or configured AccessPoint.

tools.name: reviews_ask
tools.description: Ask for a review

tools.name: reviews_accept
tools.description: Accept a review

tools.name: reviews_reject
tools.description:
Reject a review

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: debug_api
tools.description:
Debug an API on gateway instances

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: verify_api_deployment
tools.description:
Check if a deployment is possible

Check if an API deployment is possible given the API's definition and license used by the organization.

tools.name: get_api_subscribers
tools.description:
Get the API's subscribers List applications that have subscribed to a given API.
The results are paginated.

tools.name: get_api_subscriptions
tools.description:
Get the API's subscriptions

List subscriptions for a given API, always filtered by a `status` and the possibility to filter by `security`.

tools.name: create_api_subscription
tools.description:
Subscribe to an API's plan

Create a new subscription on a given API.

tools.name: export_api_subscriptions
tools.description:
Export API subscriptions as CSV

Export API subscriptions to a CSV file.

tools.name: verify_create_api_subscription
tools.description:
Check a subscription can be created

Check a subscription can be created with given API Key, and application.

tools.name: get_api_subscription
tools.description:
Get one API's subscription

Get the API's subscription by its identifier.

tools.name: update_api_subscription
tools.description:
Update one API's subscription

Update the API's subscription.

tools.name: close_api_subscription
tools.description:
Close one API's subscription

Close the API's subscription.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: pause_api_subscription
tools.description:
Pause one API's subscription

Pause the API's subscription.

tools.name: resume_api_subscription
tools.description:
Resume a paused API's subscription

Resume a previously paused subscription.

tools.name: accept_api_subscription
tools.description:
Accept an API subscription

Accept an API subscription with an optional starting / ending dates and reason.

tools.name: reject_api_subscription
tools.description:
Reject an API subscription

Reject an API subscription with an optional reason.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: transfer_api_subscription
tools.description:
Transfer an API subscription Transfer an API subscription to another plan.
The targeted plan must be published and must have the same security type. Transfer cannot be performed if general conditions apply to the targeted plan. High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: get_api_subscription_api_keys
tools.description:
Get subscription's API Keys

Get the subscription API Keys.

tools.name: renew_api_subscription_api_keys
tools.description:
Renew the subscription API Keys. Renew the subscription API Key by setting an expiration date on the existing active API Keys and creating a new API Key.
The newly created API Key is returned in response.

tools.name: update_api_subscription_api_key
tools.description:
Update a subscription API Key

Update the subscription API Key.

tools.name: revoke_api_subscription_api_key
tools.description:
Revoke a subscription API Key Revoke the subscription API Key by setting the revocation date to the current date and flagging it as revoked.
A notification will be sent to the subscriber according to his portal notification options. High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: reactivate_api_subscription_api_key
tools.description:
Reactivate subscription API Key

Reactivate the revoked or expired subscription API Key.

tools.name: get_api_primary_owner
tools.description:
Get the API's primary owner userId

Get the API Primary Owner userId. If the primary owner is a group, then the userId of the primary owner user within that group will be returned, not the groupId. The userId is wrapper into a Member object, but no other properties are returned.

tools.name: list_api_members
tools.description:
Get the API's members

List the members for a given API.

tools.name: add_api_member
tools.description:
Add a member to an API.

Add a new member to an API. Returns a 400 HTTP Error:

tools.name: update_api_member
tools.description:
Updates a member for an API.

Edit a member for an API. Returns a 400 HTTP Error:

tools.name: remove_api_member
tools.description:
Remove one API member

Remove an API member. Returns a 400 HTTP Error:

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: list_api_plans
tools.description:
List the API's plans List plans for a given API, always filtered by a `status` and the possibility to filter by `security`.
The results are paginated.

tools.name: create_api_plan
tools.description:
Create an API's plan

Create a new plan on a given API.

Plans are created in STAGING status. Publish the plan before deployment when the API should receive traffic.
Example request body: {"definitionVersion":"V4","name":"Keyless","security":{"type":"KEY_LESS"},"mode":"STANDARD","validation":"AUTO"}

tools.name: get_api_plan
tools.description:
Get one API's plan

Get the API's plan by its identifier.

tools.name: update_api_plan
tools.description:
Update one API's plan

Update the API's plan.

tools.name: patch_api_plan
tools.description:
Partially update one API's plan

Apply a partial update to an API's plan using JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396) or JSON Patch (RFC 6902). Only v4 HTTP Proxy API plans support this operation. Only the following fields are patchable: name, description, security (configuration only),

tools.name: delete_api_plan
tools.description:
Delete one API's plan

Delete the API's plan.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: close_api_plan
tools.description:
Close an API's plan

Close the API's plan.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: deprecate_api_plan
tools.description:
Deprecate an API's plan

Deprecate the API's plan.

tools.name: publish_api_plan
tools.description:
Publish an API's plan

Publish the API's plan.

tools.name: list_groups
tools.description:
List of environment groups

List the groups of a given environment.

tools.name: search_groups
tools.description:
Search for groups using filters

Search for groups using filters.

tools.name: list_group_members
tools.description:
List a group's members

List the members of a given group.

tools.name: list_integrations
tools.description:
List Integrations Get the list of Integration for a specific environment.
The results are paginated.

tools.name: create_integration
tools.description:
Create an Integration

Create a new Integration. The response body will not contain Agent Status. To get it you should use getIntegration operation.

tools.name: get_integration
tools.description:
Get a specific Integration

Get a specific Integration.

tools.name: update_integration
tools.description:
Update Integration

Update specific Integration. The response body will not contain Agent Status. To get it you should use getIntegration operation.

tools.name: delete_integration
tools.description:
Delete Integration

Delete specific integration To delete integration user must have ENVIRONMENT_INTEGRATION[DELETE] permission. Deletion is not possible if there is any federated API associated.

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: ingest_integration
tools.description:
Ingest APIs for a specific Integration

Ingest all APIs for a specific Integration.

tools.name: preview_integration
tools.description:
Preview APIs to ingest for a specific Integration

Preview APIs to ingest before actual ingestion. to perform ingestion preview.

tools.name: ingested_apis
tools.description:
Get list of ingested APIs associated to integration

Get a list of all APIs that were ingested from 3rd party provider using this integration.

tools.name: delete_ingested_apis
tools.description:
Delete ingested APIs associated to integration

Delete APIs that were ingested from 3rd party provider using this integration. To delete APIs user must have ENVIRONMENT_API[DELETE] permission. Only unpublished APIs will deleted. APIs that are published will be omitted!

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: list_integration_members
tools.description:
Get the Integration's members

List the members for a given Integration.

tools.name: add_integration_member
tools.description:
Add a member to an Integration.

Add a new member to an Integration. Returns a 400 HTTP Error:

tools.name: update_integration_member
tools.description:
Updates a member for an Integration.

Edit a member for an Integration. Returns a 400 HTTP Error:

tools.name: remove_integration_member
tools.description:
Remove one Integration member

Remove an Integration member. Returns a 400 HTTP Error:

High risk operation: require explicit user confirmation before execution.

tools.name: perform_api_analytics
tools.description:
Perform analytics on API data

Perform analytics on API data based on the provided query parameters.

tools.name: get_api_analytics_request_count
tools.description:
Get API Analytics requests count

Get API analytics request count. TCP Proxy APIs are not supported.

tools.name: get_average_messages_per_request
tools.description:
Get API Analytics average messages per request

Get API analytics average messages per request.

tools.name: get_average_connection_duration
tools.description:
Get API Analytics average connection duration

Get API analytics average connection duration. Duration is only computed for ended requests. TCP Proxy APIs are not supported.

tools.name: get_response_status_codes
tools.description:
Get API Analytics response status counts

Get API analytics response status counts. Response statuses are grouped by endpoint and status code hundreds.

tools.name: get_response_time_over_time
tools.description: Get API Analytics average response time

tools.name: get_response_status_overtime
tools.description:
Get API Analytics response status counts over time

Get API analytics response status counts over time.

tools.name: get_api_metrics_detail
tools.description:
Get API metric for a request

Get API metric for a request.

tools.name: get_api_logs
tools.description:
Get API logs

Get API logs.

tools.name: get_api_log_error_keys
tools.description:
Get API log error keys

Returns a list of distinct error keys found in connection logs for the specified API.

tools.name: get_api_log
tools.description:
Get API log for a request

Get API log for a request.

tools.name: get_api_aggregated_message_logs
tools.description:
Get API Aggregated Messages logs (with endpoint and entrypoint messages)

Get API Aggregated Messages logs (with endpoint and entrypoint messages).

tools.name: get_api_message_logs
tools.description:
Get API Messages logs

Get API Messages logs. Allow filtering.

tools.name: get_native_api_logs
tools.description:
Get native API connection logs

Returns the paginated list of native API connection log entries for the API.

tools.name: get_native_api_logs_summary
tools.description:
Get analytics for native API connection logs

Returns the count of native API connection lifecycle events grouped by connection status.

tools.name: get_native_api_log
tools.description:
Get a native API connection log for a request

Returns the native API connection log entry for the given request.

tools.name: get_api_health_average_response_time
tools.description:
Get API Health Check average response time

Get API Health Check average response time.

tools.name: get_api_health_average_response_time_overtime
tools.description:
Get API Health Check average response time overtime

Get API Health Check average response time overtime.

tools.name: get_api_availability
tools.description:
Get API availability by endpoint or by gateway

Get API Health availability.

tools.name: get_api_health_logs
tools.description:
Get API Health Check logs

Get API Health Check average response time overtime.

tools.name: get_api_audits
tools.description:
Get API Audit

Get API Audit.

tools.name: list_api_audit_events
tools.description:
List available audit event type for API

List available audit event type for API.

tools.name: get_api_events
tools.description:
Get API Events

Get API Events.

tools.name: get_api_event_by_id
tools.description:
Get single API Event by it's ID

Get specific single API Event.

tools.name: get_spec_gen_state
tools.description: Evaluate the state of the API SpecGen

tools.name: post
tools.description: Starts the API SpecGen

tools.name: trigger_api_scoring
tools.description:
Trigger API Scoring

Trigger API Scoring.

tools.name: get_api_scoring
tools.description:
Get API Scoring

Get API Scoring. Return a 404 HTTP Response status if API was never evaluated.

tools.name: get_category_apis_page
tools.description: Page of APIs within a Category

tools.name: update_category_api
tools.description: Update a specified API within a Category

tools.name: list_async_job
tools.description:
List Async Jobs

Get the list of AsyncJobs.

tools.name: generate_el
tools.description:
Generate Expression Language

Generate Expression Language.

tools.name: feedback_el
tools.description:
Submit Newt AI generated Expression Language feedback

Submit feedback for the Newt AI generated Expression Language.

tools.name: get_api_products_for_api
tools.description:
Get API Products for an API

Returns the list of API Products that include the specified API.
